articles.title: 3-vehicle crash in Scarborough sends at least 1 person to hospital
articles.summary: Toronto police are investigating a three-vehicle collision in Scarborough that sent at least one person to a hospital on Friday afternoon. Emergency crews were called to the area of Birchmount Road and McNicoll Avenue just before 12:30 p.m. Paramedics say three people were transported to a hospital; one person with serious injuries was taken to […]

articles.title: Convoy organizer Pat King guilty of intimidation, Appeal Court rules
articles.summary: The Ontario Court of Appeal has ruled that “Freedom Convoy” organizer Pat King is guilty of intimidation and has ordered a new sentencing hearing. King was charged with a number of offences for his leading role in the February 2022 demonstration that gridlocked much of downtown Ottawa for more than three weeks. He was convicted […]

articles.title: Police searching for missing senior last seen in North York
articles.summary: Toronto police are searching for a senior who has been missing since Thursday afternoon. Authorities are concerned for 79-year-old Maya who went missing from the area of Finch and Victoria Park Avenues at around 4:30 p.m. on July 16. In a news conference held Friday, investigators said a citizen came forward and reported seeing Maya […]

articles.title: Man charged with hate crimes after confrontation with ‘Today’ show’s Melvin at NBC studio
articles.summary: NEW YORK (AP) — A 40-year-old New York man faces hate crime charges following a confrontation with “Today” show host Craig Melvin at NBC’s studio in Manhattan. The man was arrested Thursday morning inside 30 Rockefeller Center in Midtown by an officer responding to reports of a disorderly individual inside the building, police said Friday. […]

articles.title: Crash between scooter, vehicle near Dufferin Mall leaves man dead: Toronto police
articles.summary: A man has died in hospital after a collision involving a motorized scooter and a sedan near Bloor Street W. and Dufferin Street Friday morning, Toronto police say.

articles.title: Mosquitos in York, Halton regions test positive for West Nile virus
articles.summary: York Region Public Health and Halton Region have confirmed some mosquitos in the regions have tested positive for West Nile virus.

articles.title: Body of Brampton man found in Stoney Creek culvert, police say
articles.summary: Hamilton police have identified a body found this week in a culvert in Stoney Creek, Ont., as a Brampton man who had also lived in Halifax. Taranpreet Singh Sidhu, 29, had immigrated to Canada from India in 2022. Anyone who had contact with him before his death are asked to contact police.

articles.title: Markham is hosting an IndyCar race next month. Is the city ready?
articles.summary: The Ontario Honda Dealers Indy at Markham will take place in the city from Friday, Aug. 14 to Sunday, Aug. 16, and the city says it’s ready to welcome the drivers, along with tens of thousands of race fans.
